## [00:00 - 03:01]

Deepash, in this video I'm going to take you through one of the key factors behind your performance on Facebook ads and the auction. So this applies to any ad which sends traffic to a website. So slightly less of relevance for capturing leads on Facebook or trying to retarget people that have viewed a percentage of your video or for app installs. But if you are relying on a website, site performance plays such a huge part in terms of how quickly the page loads and also your bounce rate as well. So I'm going to take you through some key tips that I've got directly from Facebook on what makes a landing page perform better and the factors that they take into account as well. Number one is to minimize the landing page redirects, plug-ins, link shortness and to compress large files. What does that mean? So page redirects are if you send someone to a page and it sends them off to another page that slightly slows down the experience and Facebook doesn't like that. And they're also against things like bit.ly links and that's more so because people are masking links to websites which may be against Facebook's policy and so Facebook are a bit more stringent on redirected URLs. Platforms like plug-ins, so platforms like WordPress, Shopify are prone to having plug-ins which take too long to load. Sometimes the developers are lazy, they don't optimize their code and it can slow down the loading of your page. Link shortness I mentioned, compressing large files. So for example if you've got a big image on your landing page or a video, try and get the file size as small as possible so it works super fast on desktop and mobile, on wireless and mobile network. So you'll do the best chance of someone waiting for your page to load and lots of suffering. The second one that Facebook recommend is using multi-region hosting to improve server response type. What does that mean? Multi-region hosting is something where you're using a delivery mechanism for your website and it's slightly more technical, but it uses the local region to deliver content based on what that person is. So this is more of a hosting challenge than something that you'll have control of as a marketer or as someone working on different platforms that speak to either your developer or the person behind your platform if you are getting difficulty on page load time if that's relevant. The next one is using a high quality content delivery network, CDN to reach audiences quickly. So the way a content delivery network works is that it takes your content, your creatives, your videos and files and things that would otherwise be hosted on your website, your

## [03:01 - 06:04]

clip funnels, your Shopify, your WordPress website and it hosts it on a network which is located in multiple locations across the world and it loads half quicker. So if your company, your website is hosted in a particular country, let's say the US and you have a visitor from Japan or from Australia, generally to get that data from one side of the world to the other is slower than doing it locally. So in this example, even if you're hosting in the US and someone visits from Australia, they will actually get the content that's closest to them. So it's a slightly more technical way but it's a great way of speeding up your page load time against something more from a developer's point of view or someone who's managing your website platform. Remove render blocking JavaScript. So this is another technicality. What it actually means in simple terms is when things load on your page, they will either load in parallel or not in parallel. If they load in parallel, it means item one loads and two and three and four all at the same time. So even if items one, two, three, four take one second to load, the whole page will still only take one second to load because they're load all at the same time. What a render blocking JavaScript does is nothing else will load until that particular piece of code is executed. So if that code takes three seconds to load, then your page will at minimum take three seconds to load. Even if there's five, six things that run at the same time at two seconds, the JavaScript will delay everything. So again, it's a slightly more technical thing to keep into account. But what I would recommend, some of these things are quite technical, but it's all about getting your landing page performance into the best condition it can be, is to use the free tool to just get an idea of how well your page is performing. Google have a tool called the page speed test tool. My preference is this tool from Pingdom, which I will show you now. So this example, I'm going to paste in my welcome page from my course.
